We should probably do a tool call to session.expand_file@v1 with target selector maybe "F1"? Actually target selector likely refers to a node id like "F1". The graph frontier says expand_file [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=17 priority=112 via session.walk(mode=file). So we should call session.expand_file@v1 with payload specifying target selector maybe "F1". The payload format? Not fully defined but likely includes "target_selector". Usually session.expand_file expects target selector string. Provide maybe {"target":"F1"}? Or "target_selector":"F1"? We need to follow typical usage: In previous history they used session.expand_file@v1 {status:unknown,path:,line_count:0,fields:[target]}. So they used fields like "target". So payload likely includes "target": "F1". So we can do tool_call with payload: {"target":"F1"} maybe also "depth":... but not required.

We need to output JSON with kind: "tool_call". So shape:

{

  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1","payload":{"target":"F1"}},

Thus produce JSON only.

  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}
